sutta: that's have i heard once when the venerable sariputta dwelled on mount valjoup near rajagaha he roped himself early in the morning and taking bowl and rope descended the hill with many monks in company now at a certain spot the wearable sariputta saw a large log of wood and there he addressed the monks and said see you reverence that large log of wood yes sir they replied an adapt among references 1 to mind control and if he wish view it as earth wherefore there is in that log of wood the earth element hence an adapt can view it as earth he can if he wish view it as water as fire as wind as beautiful as ugly wherefore there are references the elements of all these things in that log of wood hence an adapt 1 to mind control can view that long in such ways it's the end of the sutta
commentary: here an adapter means 1 who has finished his work meaning an arahant and the thing the pali word is edema edema is 1 who has been you could say successful because eddie 1 meaning of ed success another 1 is psychic powers so you could say 1 with psychic power or 1 with um who who has been successful in the spiritual partner but the verbal sarikuta even though he has attained all the janas he is known not to have psychic power so probably he does not refer to psychic power here he refers to an arahant 1 who has been successful in the holy path so he said no and and adapt manga and arahan can view even a lot as earth or water or fire that is heat or wind these are the 4 elements in a physical object and as beautiful or ugly this chosen that a person who has full control of his mind can do these things and actually that is why the 1 pointedness of mind now is very important samadhi in the buddha's teachings has 1 pointedness of mind is the aim of samadhi is to have control over the mind and so that as in 1 of the earlier sutas now uretna then a person can view something that is attractive as lonesome something as lonesome as even attractive and that is all because this all all these are perceptions of the mind perceptions are due to habit and perceptions can be changed if we train the mind sometimes people say that mindfulness is very important mindfulness is important but by itself it is not sufficient for example sometimes a person can know he is mindful he knows that what he is doing is wrong and yet he continues to do it why because he is not in full control of his mind for example sometimes a man or a woman may know that to commit adultery is wrong and vietnam because passion or lust controls his mind he is not he's unable to um to overcome this passion or lust so the loss of passion controls his mind rather than the mind controlling passion hence the importance of samadhi in the buddha's teachings
